summary: The current test fixture change removes the raw secret from the tip tree and the synthetic fixture fingerprint matches, but the prior BLOCKER is not fully resolved because the branch still contains the secret-bearing reviewed commit in its history despite the new review note claiming history was rebuilt.

findings.title: Secret-bearing commit still reachable from HEAD
findings.claim: The newly recorded fix says branch history was rebuilt so the raw secret never appears in any branch commit, but `git log --oneline 5cf869f..HEAD` shows only follow-up commits on top of 5cf869f and `git merge-base --is-ancestor 5cf869f HEAD` succeeds. Since 5cf869f is the last-reviewed commit that contained the raw Zoom client secret in `server/test/oauthFingerprint.test.js`, the current branch still carries the leaked secret in commit history even though the tip tree now uses a synthetic fixture.
findings.suggestion: Rebuild the feature branch from a clean pre-secret base, or squash/rebase so 5cf869f is not reachable from HEAD, then force-push. Verify with `git merge-base --is-ancestor 5cf869f HEAD` returning non-zero and a history scan over the branch showing no raw secret.
